_comment: Execution gate policy — maps task types to risk level and required gate before execution.

risk_levels.none: Read-only or fully reversible. No gate needed.
risk_levels.low: Creates or modifies data, but easily undone. Warn but allow.
risk_levels.medium: Visible to others or hard to reverse. Require preview confirmation.
risk_levels.high: Irreversible, triggers authority chain, or affects money/approvals. Require approval gate.

gates.preview: Show the agent what will happen before executing. Require explicit --confirm flag.
gates.approval: Submit a Lark Approval instance. Execution must wait for approver action.

create_document.reason: Document creation is reversible (can be deleted).

update_document.reason: Document edits are version-controlled and reversible.

write_wiki.reason: Wiki changes are versioned; rollback is available.

update_base_record.reason: Base record updates can be reverted.

write_calendar.reason: Calendar events can be deleted after creation.

send_message.reason: IM messages are immediately visible to recipients and cannot be unsent in all contexts.

create_drive_file.reason: File creation creates tenant-visible content; preview ensures correct target and permissions.

manage_wiki.reason: Structural wiki changes (moves, deletes) affect shared knowledge space.

manage_drive.reason: Drive management (moves, permission changes) affects shared file access.

manage_sheets.reason: Spreadsheet changes may affect downstream calculations or reports.

manage_slides.reason: Presentation edits may be used in live meetings.

manage_contact.reason: Contact directory changes affect identity resolution across the tenant.

create_crm_record.reason: CRM record creation creates a sales data record; wrong data is hard to clean up at scale.

send_crm_followup.reason: CRM follow-up messages are external-facing; preview content and recipient before sending.

create_expense.reason: Expense submission triggers a financial approval chain. Must not proceed without manager authorization.

submit_approval.reason: Approval submission is irreversible once submitted; approver must act to cancel.

act_approval_task.reason: Approving or rejecting an approval task affects downstream workflows and is audited.
act_approval_task.note: Agent must only act on tasks explicitly delegated to it.

manage_base.reason: Base structural changes (schema edits, table deletes) can destroy production data.
